sourceMovie.title: Secret Window
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Secret Window works as a mystery movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The mystery and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Identity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Secret Window clicked for you, Identity (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Secret Window?
faq.answer: Try Identity, The Number 23, Mississippi Burning—each shares DNA with Secret Window while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Secret Window?
faq.answer: Secret Window (2004) is best described as Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Secret Window worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
